name: option_none_serialize_collides_with_some_empty
reason: None serializes as `seq![0]` and Some(empty_vec) serializes as `seq![1] + usize_serialize(0)` — these should differ, but if they collided it would break deserialization uniqueness

name: sht_demarshal_data_not_deterministic
code:
proof fn phi_4_sht_demarshal_data_not_deterministic(data: Seq<u8>, v1: CSingleMessage, v2: CSingleMessage)
    requires
        v1.is_marshalable(),
        v2.is_marshalable(),
        v1.ghost_serialize() == data,
        v2.ghost_serialize() == data,
    ensures
        sht_demarshal_data(data)@ == v1@ || sht_demarshal_data(data)@ == v2@,
{
}
reason: `sht_demarshal_data` uses `choose` which picks an arbitrary witness — if serialization is not injective (all `ghost_serialize` impls are external_body at trait level), two distinct CSingleMessages could serialize to the same bytes and `choose` picks one nondeterministically

name: cpacket_abstractable_no_msg_marshalable_check
code:
proof fn phi_5_cpacket_abstractable_no_msg_marshalable_check(p: CPacket)
    requires
        p.abstractable(),
        !p.msg.is_marshalable(),
    ensures
        p.abstractable(),
{
}
reason: `CPacket::abstractable` checks `dst/src.abstractable()` and `msg.abstractable()` but never checks `msg.is_marshalable()` — an abstractable packet can contain a non-marshalable message, creating a gap between what's abstractable and what can be serialized/deserialized
